title: Box Breathing (4-4-4-4)
content: Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds, exhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds. Repeat 4 cycles to reduce stress and anxiety.

title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding Technique
content: Name 5 things you see, 4 things you can touch, 3 things you hear, 2 things you smell, and 1 thing you taste. This helps ground you in the present moment.

title: Reframing Negative Thoughts (CBT)
content: Identify the negative thought, gather evidence for and against it, and reframe it more realistically. Ask yourself: Is this thought based on facts or feelings?

title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
content: Tense each muscle group for 5 seconds, then release. Start with your toes and work up to your head. This reduces physical tension and promotes relaxation.

title: Sleep Hygiene Basics
content: Maintain a consistent sleep schedule, avoid screens 1 hour before bed, keep your bedroom cool and dark, and avoid caffeine after 2 PM.

title: Mindful Walking
content: Take a 10-minute walk focusing on each step, your breathing, and your surroundings. Notice colors, sounds, and sensations without judgment.

title: Journaling for Emotional Processing
content: Write for 10-15 minutes about your thoughts and feelings without censoring. This helps process emotions and gain clarity.

title: Social Connection
content: Reach out to a friend or family member, even briefly. Social connection is vital for mental health. A simple text or call can make a difference.

title: Gratitude Practice
content: Write down 3 things you're grateful for each day. This shifts focus toward positive aspects of life and can improve mood over time.

title: Setting Boundaries
content: It's okay to say no. Protect your time and energy by setting clear boundaries with others. Communicate your limits respectfully but firmly.

title: Physical Exercise for Mental Health
content: Even 20 minutes of moderate exercise can boost mood and reduce anxiety. Try walking, yoga, dancing, or any movement you enjoy.

title: Limiting Social Media
content: Set time limits on social media use. Constant comparison and information overload can increase anxiety and decrease self-esteem.

title: Self-Compassion Practice
content: Treat yourself with the same kindness you'd offer a friend. Replace self-criticism with understanding: 'I'm doing my best' or 'It's okay to struggle.'

title: Crisis Resources
content: If you're in crisis: Call 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) in the US, or contact local emergency services. You can also text 'HELLO' to 741741 (Crisis Text Line).

title: Professional Help
content: Consider speaking with a mental health professional. Therapy provides personalized support and evidence-based strategies. Many offer online sessions and sliding-scale fees.

title: Mindful Breathing
content: Focus on your breath for 2-3 minutes. Notice the sensation of air entering and leaving your body. When your mind wanders, gently return focus to your breath.

title: Breaking Tasks into Steps
content: When overwhelmed, break large tasks into tiny, manageable steps. Focus on just the next small action rather than the entire project.

title: Healthy Routine
content: Establish a daily routine with regular meal times, sleep schedule, and activities. Structure provides stability and can reduce anxiety and depression.
